steps.title: Spot the modifier
steps.content: The opening phrase "A politician from South Carolina," is a descriptive modifier. The word right after the comma must be the person being described — Elliott. Anything else creates a misplaced modifier.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Only (A) puts Elliott immediately after the comma: "A politician from South Carolina, Elliott was elected..."

(B) starts with "was the year," (C) starts with "constituents," and (D) starts with "in 1870" — none of these is Elliott.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
